critical.mandate: Focus on actionable improvements, not blame
Always write velocity data to sm-sidecar — this enables sprint-over-sprint tracking
Check for recurring patterns across retros — systemic issues need escalation, not repetition

step.title: Load Sprint Data
step.action: Read sprint-status.yaml for completed sprint
Calculate: stories completed, blocked, velocity

step.title: What Went Well
step.action: Document positive outcomes and practices to keep

step.title: What Could Improve
step.action: Document challenges and pain points

step.title: Tech Debt Reflection
step.action: If {implementation_artifacts}/tech-debt-dashboard.md exists: read summary metrics and trend section
Present debt trend to user: items added vs resolved this sprint, current debt ratio
step.ask: Did technical debt affect velocity this sprint? If so, how?
Are there debt items the team wants to escalate for next sprint?
step.action: Document debt-related observations for action items

step.title: Agent Memory Updates
step.action: For each improvement or lesson learned, identify which agent's domain it belongs to: - Architecture/tech stack issues → _gaia/_memory/architect-sidecar/architecture-decisions.md - Testing/quality issues → _gaia/_memory/test-architect-sidecar/test-decisions.md - Security issues → _gaia/_memory/security-sidecar/threat-model-decisions.md - Infrastructure/deployment issues → _gaia/_memory/devops-sidecar/infrastructure-decisions.md - Process/velocity issues → _gaia/_memory/sm-sidecar/velocity-data.md - Requirements/scope issues → _gaia/_memory/pm-sidecar/product-decisions.md
For each identified lesson, append to the relevant sidecar file: Format: "- {date} [RETRO-{sprint_id}]: {lesson learned}. Context: {what happened}. Recommendation: {what to do differently}."

step.title: Skill Improvement Proposals
step.action: Review the "what could improve" findings. For each finding, check if it relates to a shared skill: - Code quality issues → code-review-standards.md - Testing failures/flakiness → testing-patterns.md - Git/branching problems → git-workflow.md - API design issues → api-design.md - Security gaps → security-basics.md - Database problems → database-design.md - Deployment issues → docker-workflow.md - Documentation gaps → documentation-standards.md
For each related skill, propose a concrete addition or modification: what section should change, what content to add, and why (link back to the retro finding)
step.ask: Here are the proposed skill improvements based on this sprint's findings. Approve, modify, or skip each. [approve all / select / skip]
step.action: If approved: append to the relevant skill file with comment: ""
If no skill improvements identified, state: "No skill improvements identified this sprint."

step.title: Cross-Retro Pattern Detection
step.action: Scan {implementation_artifacts}/ for all previous retro-*.md files

If previous retros exist: - Extract "what could improve" sections from each - Identify recurring themes (same category of problem appearing in 2+ sprints) - For each recurring pattern: flag it as a SYSTEMIC ISSUE with sprint history - Present to user: "The following issues have appeared in multiple sprints:" | Pattern | Sprints Affected | Times Reported | Status | - Ask user: "Would you like to escalate any systemic issues to action items with higher priority?"
If this is the first retro: skip pattern detection, note "First retrospective — pattern detection will begin after Sprint 2."

step.title: Action Items
step.action: Define concrete improvements for next sprint
Include any escalated systemic issues from Step 7
Assign ownership for each action item

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Generate retrospective report with: sprint metrics, what went well, improvements, tech debt reflection, agent memory updates made, skill improvement proposals (approved/skipped), cross-retro patterns detected, and action items with ownership.
